zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Bailey於1488年出生在英國越可爾布利 在 華阿費達爾。她於大約1510年與John Arthur Mawde在英國越可爾哈利法可結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1569年在英國越可爾哈利法可享年82歲。

nameMeanings.name: Bailey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name for a steward or official, from Middle English bailli ‘manager, administrator’ (Old French baillis, from Late Latin baiulivus, an adjectival derivative of baiulus ‘attendant, carrier, porter’).
English: habitational name from Bailey in Little Mitton, Lancashire, named with Old English beg ‘berry’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

English: occasionally a topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, from Middle English (Old French) bailli ‘outer courtyard of a castle’ (Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’, a derivative of bailer ‘to enclose’). This term became a placename in its own right, denoting a district beside a fortification or wall, as in the case of the Old Bailey in London, which formed part of the early medieval outer wall of the city.

variants.name: Bailly
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Emile, Marcel, Philippe.
French: occupational name for a steward or official, from Old French baillif. Compare Bailey 4.
French: habitational name from (Le) Bailly, the name of several places in the northern part of France.

variants.name: Bale
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by the outer wall of a castle, Middle English bailli, baile, from Old French bail(le) ‘enclosure’ (see Bailey 3).
Breton (Balé): variant of Balay .
French: unexplained.

variants.name: Baily
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayly
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Bayley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

variants.name: Balay
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Rajesh, Satish.
Altered form of English   Bailey  .
French: habitational name from any of various minor places so named.

variants.name: Baile
meanings: Spanish: status name for a steward or official, from Old Spanish baile, Late Latin baiulivus; cognate with English Bailey .
French (southern): variant of   Bail  .
English: variant of   Bale  .

variants.name: Baillie
meanings: Scottish:

from Older Scots baillie, baili. In Scotland, a baillie was a status name for any of various officials, including the chief magistrate in a barony and (later) the chief magistrate or administrative officer of a barony or district. Etymologically, this is the same word as English Bailey , but the functions as well as the spelling of the two officials were different in England and Scotland.
topographic name from Older Scots bailli ‘outer court of a castle’; see Bailey 3 and Bale 1.

variants.name: Baley
meanings: English: variant of   Bailey  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Bailey was born in 1488, in Burley in Wharfedale, Yorkshire, England. She married John Arthur Mawde about 1510, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. She died about 1569, in Halifax, Yorkshire, England, at the age of 82.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Baileyは1488年にイギリスヨークシュアイアブルリー イン ウアルフエダルで生まれた。約1510年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスでJohn Arthur Mawdeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1569年にイギリスヨークシュアイアハリファックスで82歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Bailey(은)는 1488년 영국 요르크실르 버리 인 화아르페데일에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1510년 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 John Arthur Mawde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1569년 영국 요르크실르 할이파스에서 82세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Bailey родилась в 1488 г. в местности бурли ин уарфедеил, еркшир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Arthur Mawde около 1510 г. в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1569 г. в местности хэлифакс, еркшир, Англия в возрасте 82 лет.
